channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Kiro CLI Gets Worse After an Hour. Here's How I Fixed It.
item.description: I use Kiro CLI every day. Checking CloudWatch alarms, debugging IAM policies, reviewing security groups, looking at cost reports, setting up infrastructure. It is my go-to tool for anything AWS....

item.title: I Built a Persistent Memory Sidecar for My OpenClaw Agent. Here's the Code That Finally Made It Stick
item.description: Every AI agent forgets. Not metaphorically — literally. Sessions end, context compresses, and the agent wakes up fresh with no memory of what happened yesterday. For simple queries, that's fine....

item.title: Shopify App Store Submission — 14 Things I Wish I Knew Before My First Submit
item.description: I just submitted multiple Shopify app . Here's everything I learned that the docs don't clearly tell you. 1. Screencast video is REQUIRED — not optional I assumed the demo video was a "nice to...

item.title: Building a GUI from scratch in Rust — how IONA OS does compositing, widgets, and applications
item.description: ****No Qt. No GTK. No WebView. Just Rust, pixels, and a compositor. IONA OS has a GUI. A real one — with windows, buttons, scrollbars, a dock, and applications. But unlike most operating systems,...

item.title: Stop Thinking of LLMs as AI Models. Start Thinking of Them as Distributed Systems.
item.description: A few months ago, I shipped my first "production-ready" AI application. It worked beautifully — right up until real users showed up. The model wasn't the problem. The answers were good, retrieval...

item.title: Why We Built Yet Another Postgres Connection Pooler: Solving Modern Database Challenges
item.description: Originally published on tamiz.pro. Why We Built Yet Another Postgres Connection Pooler PostgreSQL remains a cornerstone of modern cloud-native applications, but its connection management systems...

item.title: "The $459/mo Grant Alert Industry Runs on a Free Government API"
item.description: Grant discovery platforms are a quiet SaaS category with remarkable pricing: $179 to $459 a month for keyword alerts on grant opportunities. The primary source for US federal grants is grants.gov,...

item.title: The ‘G-Wagen of golf carts’ could be the ideal second car
item.description: While the auto industry wrings its hands over the electric vehicle market, sweating details like aerodynamic efficiency and range anxiety, a new EV startup based in Lisbon, Portugal, is zagging in...

item.title: La IA no crea lo que tú no sabes
item.description: # Una confesión después de 5 meses de enseñar y programar con asistentes inteligentes Tengo una regla personal: nunca uses algo complejo si puedes hacerlo sencillo. Suena obvio, pero en el mundo...

item.title: AI Agents Made Me Faster. Then Attention Became the Bottleneck.
item.description: I was not trying to coin a term or build a framework. I was trying to stop being the scheduler for the agents I was working with. I started with prompts. The prompts worked. That was the problem....

item.title: ABC tells the government to get out of its newsrooms
item.description: ABC is firing back at the Federal Communications Commission after the agency opened an investigation into The View's airtime of political candidates. In a letter to the FCC on Tuesday, ABC argues...

item.title: How we built a smart AI writing agent for blog articles
item.description: Most "AI writer" products do the same thing: prompt → blob of text → paste. It works for a demo and falls apart in production, because the value isn't the prose; it's the domain expert's judgment,...

item.title: Anthropic is launching Claude Cowork on mobile and web
item.description: Starting Tuesday, Anthropic's Claude Cowork AI platform will be available on mobile and web for the first time. The expanded access is rolling out first to Max subscribers and coming to Claude...

item.title: Google announces Pixel 11 launch event in August
item.description: Google is hosting its next Made by Google launch event for Pixel hardware on August 12th in New York City, according to an invitation sent by Google to The Verge. Unusually, the event is taking...

item.title: RedWing MaaS Packages Android Bank Fraud as a Telegram Rental Service
item.description: A new Android malware operation called RedWing is being rented out on Telegram as a ready-made bank-fraud service. It lets even low-skill criminals take over a victim's phone, steal their banking...

item.title: Rogue Agent Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Hijack Google Dialogflow CX Chatbots
item.description: A critical flaw in Google's Dialogflow CX could have let an attacker with edit rights on one Code Block-enabled agent compromise other Code Block-enabled agents in the same Google Cloud project....

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.

item.title: Doom developer id reportedly cut in half as part of Xbox layoffs
item.description: As part of the mass layoffs hitting Xbox, Doom developer id Software has laid off around 50 percent of its staff, according to Game Developer. One source claimed to the publication that the cuts...

item.title: Shut Those Laptops! Anthropic Puts Its Claude Cowork Agent on Your Phone
item.description: Claude Cowork now keeps working on tasks even after you close your laptop. It’s part of a larger push toward smartphone-controlled agents.

item.title: Hugging Face Models on Foundry Managed Compute
DEBULL Tooling Abuses Microsoft Device-Code Flow to Target M365 Accounts
item.description: A Microsoft 365 device code phishing campaign has been observed leveraging collaboration-themed lures to take control of victim accounts between the last week of June 2026 and into early July, per...

item.title: Discord accidentally banned over 8,000 people for posting grids and other ‘benign’ images
item.description: Discord says a bug affecting its safety system caused it to mistakenly ban more than 8,000 accounts since May. The platform's statement follows a wave of reports from users over the past week, who...

item.title: Anker’s noise-blocking earbuds for sleeping are nearly half off
item.description: You might have a great bed and a good sleepy time routine, but if you’re still struggling to fall (or stay) asleep, Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 are worth considering. They’re designed to help...

item.title: Public GitHub Issue Could Trick GitHub Agentic Workflows Into Leaking Private Repo Data
item.description: A public issue can trick GitHub Agentic Workflows into leaking the contents of an organization's private repositories, researchers at Noma Security have shown. The attacker needs only to open a...

item.title: iRobot’s newest floor cleaner isn’t a robot
item.description: iRobot just announced its first-ever non-robotic floor cleaner. The $399 Roomba Electro Plus is a 5-in-1 hard-floor cleaner that combines vacuuming, mopping, and disinfecting, but you have to...

item.title: Microsoft fixes storage-hogging Windows 11 folder
item.description: Microsoft is addressing a Windows 11 bug that caused a folder to take up several gigabytes of storage space. As spotted earlier by Windows Latest, Microsoft included the patch in its optional June...

item.title: Court Filing Reveals Windows Device ID Helped FBI Trace Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker
item.description: U.S. prosecutors linked an alleged Scattered Spider hacker to a break-in at a luxury jewelry retailer using a persistent Windows device ID, according to a newly unsealed federal complaint....

item.title: Writer AI Flaw Could Let Agent Previews Leak Session Tokens Across Tenants
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a now-patched critical session isolation vulnerability in Writer, an enterprise generative artificial intelligence (AI) platform, that could...

item.title: These New Smart Glasses From Solos Come With a Privacy Shield for the Cameras
item.description: You can clip a cover over the cameras, which could be a double-edged sword.

item.title: Solos debuts an even lighter version of its camera-less smart glasses
item.description: Solos announced a new version of its AirGo smart glasses, one that forgoes cameras for a sleeker design and an AI assistant that relies on voice interactions. Last year's AirGo A5 weighed 36 to 40...
